title: Coffee Compound Dispersion Analysis
description: A virtual chemistry lab where the user places a coffee sample mat onto a dispersion testing apparatus to analyze how coffee compounds scatter light. The user must calibrate the instrument, position the sample, adjust wavelength and intensity parameters, run the dispersion test, and record validated results across multiple trials.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap/CDN) that implements an interactive chemistry lab for coffee compound dispersion analysis. Load both GLB assets: coffeemat.glb as a flat sample material that can be picked up and placed, and DispersionTest.glb as the main optical dispersion testing instrument on the lab bench. Scene setup: place a lab bench surface (simple box geometry, dark gray) at y=0. Position the DispersionTest apparatus at center and the coffeemat off to the side. Use a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 3, 5) looking at origin. Add ambient light (0.4 intensity), a directional light simulating overhead fluorescents, and a point light near the apparatus with slight blue tint. Add OrbitControls limited to reasonable lab viewing angles. Interaction sequence (6-8 steps with validation): Step 1: User clicks a 'Power On' HTML button to activate the apparatus (add a green emissive glow to a child mesh of DispersionTest). Step 2: User clicks a 'Calibrate' button; show a progress bar that fills over 2 seconds, then display 'Calibrated' status. Step 3: User drags the coffeemat (raycaster-based drag) onto a highlighted target zone on the apparatus (glowing plane). Validate placement is within 0.3 units of target. Snap into place on success. Step 4: User adjusts a wavelength slider (380-780nm) displayed as an HTML range input; update a colored beam (thin cylinder or line) between two points on the apparatus matching the selected wavelength color. Step 5: User adjusts an intensity slider (0-100%); scale the beam opacity accordingly. Step 6: User clicks 'Run Test'; animate the beam pulsing 3 times, then generate a random dispersion index (1.3-1.7) displayed in a results panel. Step 7: User must run at least 3 trials with different wavelengths (validate wavelength differs by >50nm each trial). Display all results in an HTML table. Step 8: Click 'Export Report' only enabled after 3 valid trials; show a summary overlay with average dispersion index. Use a status panel (HTML overlay, top-right) showing current step instructions. Disable out-of-order actions.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  apparatusPowered: boolean,         // whether the dispersion apparatus is powered on
  calibrationProgress: number,         // calibration progress from 0 to 100
  isCalibrated: boolean,         // whether the apparatus has been calibrated
  coffeematPlaced: boolean,         // whether the coffee sample mat is placed on the apparatus
  wavelengthSetting: number,         // current wavelength setting in nanometers (400-700)
  intensitySetting: number,         // light intensity setting from 0 to 100
  testRunning: boolean,         // whether the dispersion test is currently running
  testProgress: number,         // test execution progress from 0 to 100
  dispersionValue: number,         // measured dispersion coefficient result
  dataRecorded: boolean,         // whether the test results have been recorded
  stabilityTimer: number,         // elapsed time in seconds for final stability check
}
